jokes.text: It was Tres de Cinco, or the day before Cinco de Cuatro
jokes.setup: Spanish holidays have conventional naming patterns
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical fake holidays with absurd numerical progression
jokes.explanation: Classic AD wordplay - creates absurd fake holidays that sound almost plausible but make no mathematical sense

jokes.text: In case you wanted to make confetti out of something other than the pictures that they sell of the chubby Linda Ronstadt
jokes.setup: Buster bringing flowers for Cinco
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific reference to 'chubby Linda Ronstadt' pictures being used as confetti
jokes.explanation: Buster's characteristic oddly specific observations about mundane things

jokes.text: As you'll see, he admits it plenty
jokes.setup: Buster claims he couldn't admit he was desperate for a mother
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately contradicts this with meta-commentary about what audience will see
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of AD's omniscient narrator undermining characters with perfect timing

jokes.text: But his biggest fear wasn't confronting a wet, loose seal; it was losing one
jokes.setup: Buster's fear of seals (the animal)
jokes.punchline: Play on 'seal' - he's afraid of losing Lucille (a 'loose seal')
jokes.explanation: Brilliant multi-layered pun that works on literal and character levels, classic AD wordplay at its finest

jokes.text: Wait! She doesn't have her makeup! I have her lipstick! Mother!
jokes.setup: Lucille being arrested and taken away
jokes.punchline: Buster's childlike concern about makeup when his mother faces serious legal trouble
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment - focusing on trivial maternal details during crisis

jokes.text: Mother is going to come waltzing through that door any day now, and she is going to be screaming, 'Make sure you frisk that girl before...'
jokes.setup: Buster's confidence that his mother will return
jokes.punchline: His accurate prediction of Lucille's suspicious, controlling behavior toward Lupe
jokes.explanation: Shows Buster's deep knowledge of his mother's paranoid patterns

jokes.text: That's my perfume. Mm, good. Okay, good. She wouldn't wear that
jokes.setup: Buster checking if Lupe is wearing his mother's perfume
jokes.punchline: His relief that she's not, implying he's territorial about his mother's scents
jokes.explanation: Weird maternal boundary issue that's perfectly Buster

jokes.text: ♪ The vodka goes in, you pour it in the other ♪ ♪ The olive on top and then you call your... ♪ ♪ Mother. ♪
jokes.setup: Buster maintaining his mother's routine
jokes.punchline: Making up a song about martini preparation that ends with calling mother
jokes.explanation: Buster's childlike song-making about adult activities he doesn't understand

jokes.text: Except for the hair. You have reduced. Well, you're not that skinny. Welcome home, chubs. Oh, you will kill me for that. And I don't want that blood on my hands
jokes.setup: Buster talking to mannequin as if it's his mother
jokes.punchline: Having full conversation including anticipating violent reaction to calling her 'chubs'
jokes.explanation: Disturbing and funny psychological break as Buster creates elaborate fantasy conversations

jokes.text: To an uninformed observer, it looked a little like that Vince Vaughn movie Psycho
jokes.setup: Buster's disturbing behavior with mannequin
jokes.punchline: Referencing wrong actor for Psycho, implying it's the terrible 1998 remake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reference that works on multiple levels - wrong actor makes it funnier and more dismissive

jokes.text: This pasta's way overcooked, Buster! Well, you didn't leave me the simplest recipe, Mother
jokes.setup: Buster cooking for himself and the mannequin
jokes.punchline: Full argument with mannequin about cooking, including defensive responses
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the psychological break - now having full arguments with mannequin

jokes.text: Chinchilla! The one you got after Dad had the affair with the chubby intern from Riverside
jokes.setup: Playing 'Guess the Fur' game with Lucille
jokes.punchline: Connecting specific furs to specific family betrayals
jokes.explanation: Dark family dysfunction disguised as innocent game - very AD

jokes.text: Right to the smokes, huh? Give me a minute. Let me catch my breath
jokes.setup: Lucille needing to go outside for cigarettes
jokes.punchline: Asking for time to catch her breath before smoking
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contradiction - needing breath before destroying lungs

jokes.text: You've managed to combine people's fear of homosexuals and eels into one alibi
jokes.setup: Discussing Buster's testimony strategy
jokes.punchline: Impossible combination of two unrelated fears as legal defense
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd legal strategy that somehow makes perfect Arrested Development sense

jokes.text: Half your testicle was hanging out of that thing. Good! I'm glad they saw it
jokes.setup: Discussion of Buster's inappropriate costume choice
jokes.punchline: Buster's proud reaction to accidental exposure
jokes.explanation: Buster's complete lack of shame about inappropriate exposure is perfectly in character

jokes.text: And keep your ball joke to yourself, Andrew Dice Clay
jokes.setup: Buster making crude jokes about his testicle
jokes.punchline: Lucille comparing him to notoriously crude comedian
jokes.explanation: Perfect insult comparing Buster's childish crudeness to professional vulgarity

jokes.text: You're a hot mess! You're a hot mess!
jokes.setup: Argument between mother and son
jokes.punchline: Both using the exact same insult back and forth
jokes.explanation: Childish argument showing both characters' immaturity

jokes.text: Hello to you, too, 2
jokes.setup: Buster saying hello to Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Playing on her nickname by saying 'too, 2'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay on her distinguishing number

jokes.text: It's 5:00 in the afternoon. It's 5:00 a.m. somewhere
jokes.setup: Justifying day drinking with juice
jokes.punchline: Misusing the classic drinking excuse since it's the wrong time
jokes.explanation: Subverts the classic drinking excuse in the wrong direction

jokes.text: Well, that was a freebie
jokes.setup: Buster waking up with hangover
jokes.punchline: Narrator's casual dismissal of obvious juice hangover joke
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on obvious joke setup - very AD style

jokes.text: I was making two ban... Late last night, it was drizzling rain! I'm a key witness
jokes.setup: Buster arriving late and drunk to court
jokes.punchline: Incoherent attempts to explain himself while still intoxicated
jokes.explanation: Buster's complete breakdown of responsibility at crucial moment

jokes.text: The fish is frozen here! It's frozen! Do you really want to spend $30 on frozen fish?
jokes.setup: Buster asking about court verdict
jokes.punchline: Getting distracted by overpriced frozen fish instead of his mother's fate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment - priorities completely backwards during family crisis

jokes.text: Like the saltless pie?
jokes.setup: Lucille saying she's serving 3 to 5 years
jokes.punchline: Buster thinks she means like a recipe serving size
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misunderstanding showing Buster's complete disconnect from serious consequences

jokes.text: I hope your juicy blender bender was worth it
jokes.setup: Lucille angry about Buster's juice drinking
jokes.punchline: Creative alliterative insult combining juice and bender
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Lucille's wit and disdain

jokes.text: Is that baby toe of yours still crawling over the others like a weird weed?
jokes.setup: Buster trying to reconnect with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Remembering disturbing physical detail about her feet
jokes.explanation: Disgustingly specific physical detail delivered as romantic nostalgia

jokes.text: Oh, it's one of my night terrors
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 asking how he remembers her toe
jokes.punchline: It haunts his dreams as nightmare material
jokes.explanation: Dark twist - romantic memory is actually traumatic nightmare fuel

jokes.text: Did you hear it?
jokes.setup: Buster calling Lucille 2 just 'Lucille'
jokes.punchline: Narrator directly addressing audience about missing the dropped '2'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment breaking fourth wall to highlight subtle character detail

jokes.text: Before you accidentally skip back too far and wind up in the middle of the Maeby episode, trust me, no '2' was spoken
jokes.setup: Audience might rewind to check if he said '2'
jokes.punchline: Narrator acknowledging binge-watching behavior and episode structure
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on viewing habits and Netflix episode structure

jokes.text: Alex Trebek? She then rephrased it in the form of a question. Who is Alex Trebek?
jokes.setup: Buster claiming to be Alex Trebek to visit Lucille
jokes.punchline: Jeopardy format joke about rephrasing as question
jokes.explanation: Clever Jeopardy reference that works with the prison guard's reaction

jokes.text: Buster, that was innuendo. Oh. My Lord, it's like we're speaking different languages here
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 attempting seduction with food metaphors
jokes.punchline: Buster completely missing sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect illustration of Buster's sexual naivety

jokes.text: Holy Toledo, Buster, sometimes I think I'm nothing but a Horn & Hardart to you
jokes.setup: Buster only interested in food, not romance
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself to automat restaurant - dispensing food mechanically
jokes.explanation: Brilliant period-appropriate reference to automat restaurants as metaphor for their relationship

jokes.text: Buster, you can either be a son or a lover and right now, you're no good to me as a lover
jokes.setup: Relationship crisis between Buster and Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Explicitly stating the disturbing choice between maternal and sexual roles
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortably direct statement of the show's central disturbing relationship dynamic

jokes.text: What will you give me for these? All-time low
jokes.setup: Another Motherboy contestant selling awards
jokes.punchline: Pawn shop's assessment applies to both price and life situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect double meaning - both monetary value and life status assessment

jokes.text: Are you gay? No. You can tell me. I'm not. It's okay. Drew, tell him. I'm gay. Oh. Peter's gay. Oh... Sergeant Melnick's gay. Oh. Bi. Oh, sorry, he's bi
jokes.setup: Army's new acceptance policy
jokes.punchline: Overwhelming cascade of sexual orientation revelations overwhelming Buster
jokes.explanation: Rapid-fire revelations that overwhelm Buster and satirize military policy changes

jokes.text: Who did Larry Kert replace in Company? I don't know. I know who he thought he was replacing, and it was Dean Jones. But he didn't come close
jokes.setup: Officer trying to prove military credentials
jokes.punchline: Lucille's incredibly specific Broadway trivia knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - obscure cultural knowledge delivered with authority

jokes.text: He deserves a big hand for that and we gave him one
jokes.setup: Buster injured in military service
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of applause and literal prosthetic hand
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pun that's both funny and darkly appropriate to the situation

jokes.text: This is so much more fun than 'Guess the Fur'
jokes.setup: Buster enjoying drone piloting
jokes.punchline: Comparing warfare to his childhood games with mother
jokes.explanation: Dark callback showing Buster's inability to distinguish between games and reality

jokes.text: If I had him back in the '60s, Cuba would be the 51st state
jokes.setup: Praising Buster's drone piloting skills
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Bay of Pigs invasion failure
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference to military failures delivered casually

jokes.text: Take that, Taliban wedding!
jokes.setup: Buster playing drone warfare like video game
jokes.punchline: Gleefully targeting civilian celebration
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny commentary on drone warfare and civilian casualties

jokes.text: Oh, you can go to Spain in this thing? Maybe you should take a break
jokes.setup: Buster accidentally bombing Spanish museum
jokes.punchline: His excitement about travel possibilities rather than destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster response - misses the gravity while excited about wrong thing

jokes.text: Innocent people?! You mean I've been hurting guilty people?!
jokes.setup: Officer warning about hurting innocent people
jokes.punchline: Buster's horror at realizing he's been hurting anyone, but relief they were guilty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant moment showing Buster's innocence and the military's deception

jokes.text: This looks like you're pointing to a place that buys your gold
jokes.setup: Buster's large prosthetic hand
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to pointing gesture in gold buying ads
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comparison to cheesy commercial imagery

jokes.text: It's as if they wrote that song for my own son
jokes.setup: Lucille calling Buster 'hopelessly hopeless'
jokes.punchline: Referencing song as if it was written about her personal situation
jokes.explanation: Lucille's narcissistic tendency to make everything about her

jokes.text: Too hard. Uh, I think that was too hard. Okay. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Buster learning to control prosthetic hand
jokes.punchline: Repeated failures with overly gentle acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy sequence with repetitive structure - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Hope this guy likes the music acts on Saturday Night Live because he's gonna be watching a lot of them
jokes.setup: Buster's poor TV remote control skills
jokes.punchline: Implication he'll be stuck watching musical guests because he can't change channels
jokes.explanation: Funny specific consequence of his prosthetic hand problems

jokes.text: Aren't you the 'cu-test'?
jokes.setup: Buster reacting to cute bunny
jokes.punchline: Revealing the 'Q-test' is actually about cuteness, not intelligence
jokes.explanation: Clever reveal of what the military test was actually measuring

jokes.text: I see a veteran whose hand I'd like to shake. Not that one
jokes.setup: Ophelia's positive reaction to meeting veteran Buster
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction about which hand to avoid
jokes.explanation: Quick visual gag about the impractical prosthetic

jokes.text: I suppose we could use a Blind Side Monster. Well, we shouldn't call him a monster
jokes.setup: Herbert agreeing to help Buster
jokes.punchline: Calling him monster while Ophelia objects, then continuing to call him monster
jokes.explanation: Herbert's immediate contradiction of his wife's kindness

jokes.text: See, light drizzle will just annoy you but, you see, thunder... will 'F' you up!
jokes.setup: Buster explaining weather to bullies
jokes.punchline: Absurd meteorological intimidation with censored profanity
jokes.explanation: Buster's childlike attempt at intimidation through weather metaphors

jokes.text: Whether he's an attention hog who looks just like you, or whether he's half your size
jokes.setup: Cop praising Buster for standing up to bullies
jokes.punchline: Describing types of bullies that exactly match current situation
jokes.explanation: Ironic description that matches the exact bullying situation

jokes.text: If you want me to pay for her diaphragm, I get to put my hand in her... Shoe! Nice stop, Blind Side Monster
jokes.setup: Herbert making inappropriate political analogy
jokes.punchline: Buster's innocent save from sexual reference to shoe reference
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection save, though setup is a bit obvious

jokes.text: I've worn a captain's hat with someone who had hair like that
jokes.setup: Seeing photo of Herbert with prostitute
jokes.punchline: Innocent reference to his own encounter with prostitute
jokes.explanation: Callback delivered with Buster's characteristic innocence

jokes.text: You can either be a son or a lover to me. And I already have two sons
jokes.setup: Ophelia explaining relationship options to Buster
jokes.punchline: Echoing Lucille 2's earlier ultimatum while rejecting son option
jokes.explanation: Direct callback with twist - she already has sons so only lover option available

jokes.text: Hamster or turtle? No, Mother. Hamsters are gross. It's a female. Human. Female human. Whole thing
jokes.setup: Buster telling Lucille he's in love
jokes.punchline: Lucille's assumption it's a pet, Buster's overly specific clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Lucille's low expectations and Buster's defensive overcompensation

jokes.text: I'm not a mother boy anymore. I'm a mother man
jokes.setup: Buster declaring his independence from mother
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to sound mature while still defining himself through mother relationship
jokes.explanation: Buster's failed attempt at maturity still defines him through maternal relationship

jokes.text: I'm not a mother boy anymore. I'm a mother man.
jokes.setup: Buster declaring his growth and independence
jokes.punchline: His declaration of maturity still revolves around 'mother'
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Buster's character - even his growth is defined by his mother fixation

jokes.text: After I've zipped down, you can't expect me to zip up!
jokes.setup: Buster begging Ophelia not to leave him
jokes.punchline: Sexual metaphor delivered with complete innocence about what he's saying
jokes.explanation: Buster accidentally making sexual metaphor while being completely innocent

jokes.text: And he's gotten himself in some pretty hot ham water this time
jokes.setup: Buster describing Herbert's scandal
jokes.punchline: Malapropism combining 'hot water' with 'ham' for no reason
jokes.explanation: Classic Buster malapropism - adding unnecessary food reference to idiom

jokes.text: Oh, this is just like Mother's Day Eve!
jokes.setup: Buster drinking juice and partying
jokes.punchline: Comparing debauchery to innocent holiday celebration
jokes.explanation: Buster's innocent comparison of wild party to wholesome family celebration

jokes.text: Perhaps uno mas, señor, to feed to the fishes? Gene Parmesan. This is not my real job
jokes.setup: Person offering to feed fish at Cinco
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's Gene Parmesan in disguise, and this undercover work isn't his real job
jokes.explanation: Classic Gene Parmesan reveal with additional joke about this not being his real job

jokes.text: Oh, no. I'm in the movie. Oh, no
jokes.setup: Buster realizing he's been caught on camera
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about being in the show/movie
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where character acknowledges being filmed for the show
